duration_days.unit: days to first harvest (1 yr) / primary production (3 yr)

description.en: Found in virtually every Sri Lankan home garden — an indispensable culinary herb in Sri Lankan, Indian, and Bangladeshi kitchens. Evergreen small tree; leaves harvestable year-round in warm climates. First harvest possible ~1 year after planting; primary commercial production begins after 3 years. Prefers well-drained soil kept moist, full sun or partial shade, sheltered from wind. Regular pruning promotes bushier growth and higher leaf yield.

temperature_c.quote: growth is more robust when temperatures are at least 18 °C

duration_days.quote: Leaves can be harvested about one year after planting; primary production starts after three years

description.quote: The curry leaf tree, known as karapincha, is a plant found in almost all home gardens in Sri Lanka and is an indispensable ingredient in local kitchens
